title: rubus radula subsp. ericetorum
intro: Rubus radula subsp. ericetorum, also known as the heather bramble or heather dewberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus radula plant, which is a type of wild raspberry native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. The health benefits of Rubus radula subsp. ericetorum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall health and well-being. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments.
